HEB.1.en: The letter opens with a majestic declaration: Jesus is the ultimate and final word of God — superior to angels, the exact imprint of God's nature, sustaining the universe by his powerful word.
HEB.1.zh: 书信以宏大的宣告开篇：耶稣是神最终极的话语——远超天使，是神本体的真像，用他权能的命令托住万有。

HEB.2.en: Why did the Son become human? To taste death for everyone, to destroy the devil's power over death, and to become a merciful high priest who fully identifies with our weakness.
HEB.2.zh: 神的儿子为何成为人？为要替众人尝死味，败坏魔鬼掌死权的能力，并成为能体恤我们软弱的慈悲大祭司。

HEB.3.en: Jesus is greater than Moses — as a Son over God's house, not merely a servant in it. The warning is urgent: don't harden your heart like Israel in the wilderness, or you will miss God's rest.
HEB.3.zh: 耶稣比摩西更大——他是神家的儿子，而非仅为仆人。警告急切：不可硬心，如同以色列人在旷野，否则必错失神的安息。

HEB.4.en: A Sabbath rest remains for God's people. The key to entering is faith. God's word is living and active, piercing to the division of soul and spirit. Our great high priest sympathises with every weakness.
HEB.4.zh: 神的子民仍有安息日的安息存留。进入的关键是信心。神的道是活泼有功效的，能刺入剖开。我们的大祭司能体恤一切软弱。

HEB.5.en: Jesus qualifies as high priest not by self-appointment but by divine calling, in the order of Melchizedek. He learned obedience through suffering — and became the source of eternal salvation for all who obey him.
HEB.5.zh: 耶稣成为大祭司，不是自己取得，而是神的呼召，照麦基洗德的等次。他藉苦难学了顺从，成了永远得救的根源。

HEB.6.en: A sobering warning against falling away, followed by great encouragement: God's promise to Abraham is an anchor for the soul, sure and steadfast, reaching into the inner place behind the curtain where Jesus has gone as forerunner.
HEB.6.zh: 严肃警告离弃之危，随即是极大鼓励：神对亚伯拉罕的应许如同灵魂的锚，坚固牢靠，通入幔内，耶稣已作先锋进入其中。

HEB.7.en: Melchizedek — priest-king of Salem — prefigures Jesus perfectly: without genealogy, without end of life, receiving tithes even from Abraham. Jesus holds his priesthood permanently, always living to intercede.
HEB.7.zh: 麦基洗德——撒冷的祭司王——完美预表耶稣：无族谱、无命之终，连亚伯拉罕都给他纳十分之一。耶稣的祭司职任永不更换，长远活着代求。

HEB.8.en: Jesus ministers in the true heavenly tabernacle, not the earthly shadow. He is the mediator of a new covenant — the one Jeremiah prophesied, where God's law is written on hearts, not stone.
HEB.8.zh: 耶稣在真正天上的帐幕中供职，而非地上的影像。他是新约的中保——耶利米所预言的：神的律法写在心上，而非石版。

HEB.9.en: The old tabernacle and its rituals were copies of heavenly realities. Christ entered the true holy place once for all with his own blood — securing an eternal redemption that animal sacrifices could never accomplish.
HEB.9.zh: 旧约的帐幕和礼仪不过是天上实物的影像。基督用自己的血，一次进入真圣所，成就了永远的救赎——这是动物祭祀永不能成就的。

HEB.10.en: The repeated sacrifices of the law could never perfect worshippers. But Christ offered one sacrifice for sins forever, then sat down. Now we draw near with full assurance, hold fast our confession, and stir one another to love and good works.
HEB.10.zh: 律法一再重复的祭物永不能使人完全。但基督一次献上赎罪祭就坐下了。如今我们坦然进到神面前，坚守承认，彼此激励行善。

HEB.11.en: The great hall of faith. From Abel to the prophets, ordinary men and women trusted God's promises they could not yet see — and their lives were transformed. Faith is the substance of things hoped for, the evidence of things not seen.
HEB.11.zh: 信心的伟大殿堂。从亚伯到众先知，普通男女信靠神尚未看见的应许——生命因此改变。信就是所望之事的实底，是未见之事的确据。

HEB.12.en: Hebrews 12 opens with one of the most stirring calls to perseverance in the entire New Testament. Building on the great hall of faith in chapter 11, the author urges believers to run their race with endurance — eyes fixed on Jesus, who himself endured the cross. The chapter reframes suffering not as abandonment but as the Father's discipline, the mark of a true son or daughter. It closes with a breathtaking contrast: the terrifying Sinai of the old covenant versus the heavenly Zion of the new, an unshakeable kingdom received by grace.
HEB.12.zh: 希伯来书第十二章以新约中最激励人心的坚忍呼召之一开篇。作者在第十一章信心英雄的基础上，催促信徒存心忍耐，奔那摆在前头的路程——目光定睛于耶稣，那位亲自忍受十字架的主。本章将苦难重新诠释，不是被遗弃，而是天父的管教，是真儿女的印记。最后以震撼人心的对比作结：旧约令人恐惧的西奈山，对比新约中荣耀的天上锡安山，那不能震动的国度，唯靠恩典领受。

HEB.13.en: The final chapter of Hebrews is a collection of practical exhortations: brotherly love, hospitality, remembering prisoners, honouring marriage, freedom from the love of money. The anchor is the unchanging Christ: 'Jesus Christ is the same yesterday, today and forever.' We offer a sacrifice of praise through him, going outside the camp to bear his reproach.
HEB.13.zh: 希伯来书最后一章是一系列实际劝勉：弟兄相爱、款待客旅、记念被囚的人、尊重婚姻、不贪爱钱财。根基是那永不改变的基督："耶稣基督昨日、今日、一直到永远是一样的。"我们藉着他献上颂赞为祭，出到营外忍受他所受的凌辱。

2JN.1.en: The shortest letter in the New Testament — just 13 verses — is a masterclass in clarity. Written by 'the elder' (almost certainly the Apostle John) to a local church and its members, it celebrates those walking in truth, renews the ancient command to love one another, and sounds a sharp warning: do not welcome false teachers who deny the Incarnation into your home or even greet them, lest you share in their evil work. Truth and love are inseparable twins throughout.
2JN.1.zh: 这是新约最短的书信，仅十三节，却是一篇精炼的劝勉。作者"长老"（几乎可确定是使徒约翰）写给一间地方教会及其成员，称赞那些遵行真理的人，重申彼此相爱的古老命令，并发出严肃警告：不要接待否认道成肉身的假师傅，甚至不可问安，免得在他们的恶行上有分。真理与爱心始终相辅相成，贯穿全篇。

JHN.1.en: John's Gospel opens not with a birth story but with eternity: 'In the beginning was the Word.' Jesus is the eternal Logos — the Word through whom all things were made — who became flesh and dwelt among us. John the Baptist is introduced solely as a witness pointing to the true Light. The chapter closes with the first disciples: Andrew, Peter, Philip, and Nathanael, each finding Jesus in their own way. The famous verse 1:14 — 'the Word became flesh' — is the hinge on which all of Christian theology turns.
JHN.1.zh: 约翰福音开篇不是诞生故事，而是永恒：太初有道。耶稣是永恒的道——万物藉他而造——他成了肉身，住在我们中间。施洗约翰的唯一使命是为真光作见证。本章末尾记载了最初的门徒：安得烈、彼得、腓力和拿但业，各以不同的方式来到耶稣面前。第14节——道成了肉身——是整个基督教神学的枢纽。

JHN.2.en: John 2 holds two unforgettable scenes. First, the wedding at Cana: Jesus turns water into wine at his mother's quiet prompting — his first sign, revealing his glory and prompting his disciples' faith. Then the temple clearing: Jesus drives out the merchants and money-changers with a whip, declaring the temple his Father's house. When challenged, he speaks of raising 'this temple' in three days — a saying the disciples only understood after the resurrection. Both signs point to the same truth: the old order is being replaced by something new, and Jesus himself is the centre of it.
JHN.2.zh: 约翰福音第二章记载了两个令人难忘的场景。首先是迦拿婚宴：耶稣在母亲的轻声提示下，将水变为酒——这是他所行的第一件神迹，显出他的荣耀，门徒因此信了他。其次是洁净圣殿：耶稣用鞭子赶出牛羊和兑换银钱的人，宣告殿是他父的殿。当人们质疑时，他说要在三天内重建这殿——这句话直到复活后门徒才明白。两个神迹指向同一真理：旧的秩序正在被新的所取代，而耶稣自己就是一切的中心。
